Title: Plastidic pyruvate dehydrogenase complex E1 component subunit Alpha1 is involved in galactolipid biosynthesis required for amyloplast development in rice

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: plastidic pyruvate dehydrogenase complex E1 component subunit alpha1
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Plastidic pyruvate dehydrogenase–fatty acid–galactolipid biosynthesis pathway linked to amyloplast starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastid acetyl-CoA supply for de novo fatty acid and galactoglycerolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the E1 alpha subunit of plastidic pyruvate dehydrogenase complex that catalyzes pyruvate oxidative decarboxylation together with other PDC subunits to generate acetyl-CoA for plastid fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Galactolipids are essential membrane lipids for amyloplast development; starch is the major storage carbohydrate determining kernel texture, grain weight and nutritional quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes galactolipid accumulation, proper amyloplast development and starch biosynthesis; loss of function causes floury endosperm and reduced grain weight, while overexpression increases grain size and yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLO19 was cloned as LOC_Os04g02900 encoding plastidic PDC E1 alpha1, and EMS mutation caused floury endosperm with abnormal compound starch granules. 2. Complementation restored normal endosperm, and CRISPR knockout recapitulated the mutant phenotype. 3. flo19 showed reduced plastidic PDC activity and decreased MGDG and DGDG contents in developing endosperm. 4. FLO19 localizes to plastids/amyloplasts and interacts with ptPDC-E1-beta1 and beta2 as part of the ptPDC complex. 5. Overexpression increased grain length, width, 1000-grain weight and grain yield per plant. 6. A promoter haplotype pFLO19L was associated with higher expression and longer grain length.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; genetic complementation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization by GFP; ptPDC enzyme assay; lipid quantification; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; IP-MS; overexpression; haplotype association; promoter luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tissues, with higher expression in stems, leaf sheaths and panicles; also expressed in developing endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed broadly in rice tissues and developing endosperm at 6–21 DAF; overexpression elevated transcript in 9-DAF endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds/endosperm at 6, 9 and 12 DAF; brown grain stage
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native FLO19 promoter (complementation, GUS); maize Ubiquitin promoter (overexpression and in vivo GFP line); CaMV 35S promoter (transient localization, Co-IP, IP-MS constructs)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduced plant height, slowed growth, lowered total starch, amylose and protein, reduced grain size and 1000-grain weight, and increased lipid content; overexpression showed no major penalty in plant height, tiller number, panicle length or seed setting rate, except altered grain number per panicle
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving grain size, grain weight and yield by overexpression or promoter editing/selection of the pFLO19L haplotype; useful for breeding longer grains with minimal reported agronomic penalties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causative natural variation is a 4-bp promoter indel associated with expression and grain length. Mutation altered expression of many starch synthesis genes, likely indirectly through defective amyloplast development and lipid supply.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS identified ptPDC complex members; 3K rice genome panel sequence variation used for association analysis (specific accession NA)

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: plastidic pyruvate dehydrogenase complex E1 component subunit beta1
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Plastidic pyruvate dehydrogenase pathway supplying acetyl-CoA for fatty acid and galactolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Pyruvate-derived E1 reaction intermediates within ptPDC
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the E1 beta subunit of the plastidic pyruvate dehydrogenase complex together with E1 alpha1 in pyruvate oxidative decarboxylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports membrane lipid formation needed for amyloplast development and starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Component of ptPDC complex interacting with FLO19; implicated in precursor supply for galactolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ptPDC-E1-beta1 was identified as a plastid-localized rice ptPDC-E1 beta subunit. 2. It interacted with FLO19/ptPDC-E1-alpha1 and ptPDC-E1-beta2 in Y2H, BiFC and Co-IP assays. 3. It was detected in ptPDC-E1-alpha1 IP-MS, supporting its inclusion in the ptPDC complex.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; subcellular localization; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; IP-MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed throughout the plant with a distinct pattern
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be considered for multigene engineering of plastid acetyl-CoA supply, but no direct trait engineering evidence was provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the main mutant gene; included as a core complex component directly examined in Results.
